name: TAO Failed Breakdown Hold v1
thesis: TAO 4h is the structurally different lane for testing a failed breakdown reclaim that must hold before continuation entry. The edge should come from rejection of downside acceptance, then continuation only after the reclaim proves durable.
invalidation_condition: Abort if failed-breakdown setups are too rare, if hold confirmation conflicts with the reclaim logic, or if the continuation leg never develops after the hold.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price breaks below local support, reclaims it, and only becomes tradable when that reclaimed level actually holds on the next bar sequence.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: In choppy markets, breakdowns often fail. The reclaim-plus-hold filter tries to convert that failure into a continuation entry without taking the first reactive bounce blindly.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: TAO 4h is a useful structurally different lane because failed-breakdown behavior should be more obvious on a volatile asset, while the 4h frame still allows clear hold/reclaim structure.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If failed breakdowns only mean-revert to balance and do not continue, the hold-confirmed continuation entry will still be too late.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If failed-breakdown setups appear but continuation is brief, move the first profit target closer before tightening the reclaim condition.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; reclaim, hold, and DI confirmation are sequential
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by broad breakdown/reclaim rules instead of exact candle-pattern requirements

long: Time-based de-risk after 3 bars if unrealized gain < 0.2 * ATR_14
short: Time-based de-risk after 3 bars if unrealized gain < 0.2 * ATR_14
